content:
Letting $\deg f=d$, the left‐hand side has degree $d$ while the right has degree $2d-1$, so $d=1$.  Write $f(x)=Ax+B$.  Then
\[
f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)=3Ax+3B,
\quad
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013x}=\frac{A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2x+2AB+\frac{B^2}{x}}{2013}.
\]
Multiplying through by $2013x$ gives
\[
6039Ax^2+6039Bx = A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2,
\]
so comparing coefficients yields $A=6039$ and $B=0$.  Hence
\[
f(x)=6039x,
\]
and $f(1)=6039$.  Thus the sum of all possible values of $f(1)$ is
\[
\boxed{6039}.
\]

f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)=3Ax+3B,
\quad
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013x}=\frac{A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2x+2AB+\frac{B^2}{x}}{2013}.

Multiplying through by $2013x$ gives
\[

\]
so comparing coefficients yields $A=6039$ and $B=0$.  Hence
\[
f(x)=6039x,
\]
and $f(1)=6039$.  Thus the sum of all possible values of $f(1)$ is
